extraction.one_line_summary: DE GENDT Thomas won from a 21-rider breakaway after FONDA POOL attacked at 46km to go, dropping most of the break before DE GENDT outsprinted the final quartet.
tactical_patterns: LÓPEZ Juan Pedro personally marked KÄMNA Lennard's pink jersey attack with 7.5km remaining, refusing team domestique control despite 38-second deficit
FONDA POOL Mathieu's 46km attack fractured 21-rider break into final quartet, revealing sprinters couldn't bridge despite organized chase
Trek-Segafredo controlled peloton for LÓPEZ Juan Pedro at 4:06 deficit, unwilling to allow 21-rider break any GC threats free rein

rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: won from four-up sprint after surviving FONDA POOL's 46km selection, first Grand Tour stage in three years
rider_signals.FONDA POOL Mathieu: neutral: strong enough to fracture break at 46km but couldn't sustain to finish, caught by chasers inside final 10km
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: negative: 38-second GC deficit prompted 7.5km attack but LÓPEZ Juan Pedro marked personally, suggesting hesitation from Trek tactics
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: personally marked KÄMNA's attack rather than using domestiques despite pink jersey, showing unexpected defensive strength
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: fourth top-five finish suggests consistent form, descended well enough to nearly bridge 26-second gap with FONDA POOL
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: negative: lost 3:33 to break survivors while main GC peloton lost only 3:00, isolated positioning cost 33 seconds
rider_signals.VON HOEEK: positive: controlled final 3-4km for quartet sprint, tactical awareness from aggressive positioning earlier
rider_signals.GABBURO Davide: neutral: strong enough to reach final quartet after FONDA POOL selection but lacked sprint to contest podium
rider_signals.ARCAS Jorge: neutral: chased solo to bridge after GABBURO move, made final four but no sprint speed against DE GENDT
